name: Pork in Balsamic Vinegar
recipeInstructions:
TENDERLOIN
1.5 lb Boneless pork tenderloin
2 Tbsp Olive oil; divided
Salt and pepper to taste
2/3 cup Dry white wine; divided
For the marinade:
1. In a skillet, combine onion and cider vinegar and simmer until the onion is soft.
2. Add rosemary, sage, parsley, balsamic vinegar, juice of 1/2 lemon, pink peppercorns, and extra-virgin olive oil.
3. Stir well, remove from heat, and set aside.
4. Pat pork with paper towels and rub it all over with 1 Tbsp olive oil.
5. Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste.
6. In a frying pan, heat 1 Tbsp olive oil over medium-high heat and brown the meat on all sides.
7. Transfer the meat to a baking dish and add 1/3 cup dry white wine to the dish.
8. Roast for 20 to 25 minutes, or until cooked to 155°F to 160°F.
9. Midway through cooking, add another 1/3 cup wine to the pan.
10. Transfer the meat to a deep non-metal dish just large enough to hold it.
11. Pour the marinade over the meat and let it marinate, covered in the refrigerator for at least 3 hours, or up to 3 days.
12. Bring the meat, in the marinade, to room temperature before serving.
13. Cut into thin slices, arrange on a serving platter, and spoon some of the marinade over the slices.
Of course, this dish can also be reheated in the marinade and served warm.
